# V9 Algorithmic Models Changelog

This version adds a full algorithmic mechanism layer.

## New folders

- `26_ALGORITHMIC_MECHANISM_MODELS/`
- `27_ALGORITHM_SIMULATION_LAB/`
- `28_MATHEMATICAL_PROOF_SKETCHES/`
- `29_ALGORITHM_READY_SLIDES/`
- `30_ALGORITHMIC_RESEARCH_AGENDA/`

## Core upgrade
The vault now explains how manufactured traction can work inside ranking/recommender-like systems through:

- pairwise rank flips;
- two-stage candidate/ranking bottlenecks;
- multi-behaviour proxy vectors;
- exposure-engagement feedback loops;
- explore/exploit seed bias;
- social proof and threshold cascades;
- popularity bias and preferential attachment;
- coordination graph/null models;
- profile-injection recommender attacks;
- budget/attribution pollution;
- algorithmic dampening and countermeasures.

## Safe framing
The models do not reveal private platform algorithms. They explain mechanism-level vulnerabilities of any system that allocates attention or trust using manipulable proxies.
